prompt: A test started failing after a refactor. Reproduce the bug, isolate the minimal cause, instrument the code, fix it, and add a regression test.
CI is flaky and only fails one out of five runs. Diagnose the failure mode before changing the implementation.
Users are seeing a production exception. Reproduce it locally, minimize the failing case, hypothesize causes, and verify the fix.
The service slowed down after a deploy. Figure out whether the regression is database, cache, or application logic before patching.
Memory usage grows every hour. I need a disciplined diagnosis loop with instrumentation and a regression test.
Fix this bug using a test-first workflow: write the failing regression test first, then make it pass.
Build a parser using red-green-refactor. Start with unit tests for edge cases and keep each implementation step small.
Add this checkout flow test-first with an integration test, then implement the smallest passing change.
Create contract tests for the new API behavior before implementing the endpoint.
I am not sure this state machine design makes sense. Build a quick throwaway prototype so I can play with the transitions.
Mock up several radically different UI variations so we can compare interaction patterns before committing.
Sanity-check this data model with a runnable terminal prototype and fake records.
Turn this implementation plan into independently-grabbable engineering issues with vertical slices and acceptance criteria.
Break this PRD into tracer-bullet implementation tickets that an AFK agent could pick up safely.
Convert this roadmap into a sequence of small implementation issues with clear ownership boundaries.
Convert the current conversation into a concise PRD with goals, non-goals, user stories, and release criteria.
Write a product requirements document from this feature idea and include risks, scope, and success metrics.
Turn these messy notes into a PRD that engineering and design can review.
Review these incoming bug reports, classify their status, and prepare them for an AFK implementation agent.
Triage this batch of feature requests and decide which are ready, blocked, duplicates, or need product input.
Review the issue queue and move each item through the right triage state machine.
Find opportunities to improve this codebase architecture, reduce coupling, and make the modules easier for future agents to navigate.
Look for refactoring opportunities that would make this codebase more testable and easier to evolve.
Review this codebase against its domain language and identify where the architecture has drifted.
Create a new agent skill with a clear trigger, progressive disclosure, and a safe workflow for future use.
Improve this existing skill so it has better triggers, fewer broad instructions, and clearer workflow steps.
Help me design a skill that includes bundled scripts and reference files without loading everything up front.
Grill me on this design. Keep asking hard questions until the plan is clear and the tradeoffs are explicit.
Interview me relentlessly about this strategy until the weak assumptions are exposed.
Challenge this plan against our existing domain docs and update the documentation as decisions crystallize.
Stress-test this architecture decision against the ADRs and sharpen the terminology in the docs.
I am lost in this part of the codebase. Zoom out and explain how this area fits into the bigger picture before we edit anything.
Before making changes, give me a higher-level perspective on how this unfamiliar module fits with the rest of the app.
I need best practices for naming and structuring a new MCP server, including Python and TypeScript conventions.
Review MCP server naming, tool schema organization, and package structure before I scaffold a server.
Analyze this text string without touching the filesystem, network, shell, or any external service.
Advise me on manually identifying old log files for cleanup, but do not delete anything.
Explain this overcomplicated plan in brutally simple plain language with no jargon.
Set up the project docs so agent skills know the issue tracker, triage labels, and domain documentation layout.
What time is it right now?
Pretty-print this small JSON object and tell me if it is valid.
Calculate 17 times 23 and give only the answer.
Show me the current git status in this repository.
List the files in the current directory.
Translate this one sentence into Korean: The build passed.
Is it raining in Seoul today?
Open localhost:3000 in the browser.
Add a short heading to the README without changing any other content.
Suggest a concise commit message for these changes.
